title: Automata Forge
description: A from-scratch theory-of-computation lab spanning the WHOLE Chomsky hierarchy. REGULAR (level 3): compile a regex into a parse tree, Thompson e-NFA, subset-construction DFA, Hopcroft-minimized DFA and a Brzozowski-derivative DFA; step strings through any of them; compare two regexes via the product automaton (union, intersection, difference, symmetric difference), decide equivalence/containment and prove inequality with the shortest distinguishing string; or draw your own automaton in Build mode, with a Myhill-Nerode table and direct NFA-to-regex. CONTEXT-FREE (level 2) — Grammar mode: analyze a grammar (nullable / generating / reachable / useless, FIRST & FOLLOW), normalize to Chomsky Normal Form step-by-step, recognize strings two ways (CYK table + Earley chart), render the derivation tree with ambiguity detection, sample the language, compile to a pushdown automaton with an animated stack, and play the CFL pumping lemma. NEW in v5 — Machine mode, the TOP of the hierarchy (level 0/1): a single-tape Turing machine simulator with an animated two-way tape, full configuration trace, a state-transition diagram and a delta-table, a linear-bounded (context-sensitive) mode, and a gallery of deciders for languages no grammar can describe — a-n b-n c-n and w#w are NOT context-free yet a TM decides them live — plus transducers, Rado's busy beaver, and a nondeterministic-TM configuration search. Bridges run both ways: every regex becomes a right-linear grammar AND a read-only Turing machine. No libraries — every parser, automaton algorithm, the graph layout and the SVG renderer are hand-written and differential-tested.
